$schema: Allowlist for check-no-silent-rep-clears.mjs (v3 — AST-detected, content-anchored, counted). Each entry = { file, field, anchor, count, snippet, reason }. `anchor` is a hash of the clear statement + up to 2 preceding non-blank lines; it survives unrelated line shifts but changes if the clear's own surrounding code changes (forcing re-review). `count` caps how many identical-anchor occurrences are allowed (default 1) so a pasted duplicate still flags. Adding an entry means you've verified the clear is intentional, cannot drop a user's picked value in a real submission flow, and has been reviewed by someone other than the author. NOTE: only `blitzId` clears appear here — `setterId`/`repId` are never reactively cleared (that's the regression this guard prevents).

entries.reason: repId-change effect — blitz availability is rep-scoped, so an old blitz must be cleared when the rep changes. setterId is preserved (validator catches mismatches).
Admin's closer picker — the chosen blitz may no longer match the new closer's blitz list. setterId is explicitly NOT cleared (was the regression source).
Lead-source toggle off `blitz` — the blitz <select> only renders when leadSource === 'blitz', so an orphan blitzId would persist invisibly. setterId preserved.
Mobile equivalent of the desktop repId-change effect. Clears the rep-scoped blitz when the rep changes; setterId preserved.

Mobile admin Closer (Rep) picker — clears blitzId when the closer changes because blitz availability is rep-scoped; the new closer may not be approved for the prior blitz. setterId is NOT cleared (picker even filters the setter out of closer options).

Mobile lead-source toggle off `blitz`. Same rationale as the desktop lead-source toggle — an orphan blitzId would persist invisibly. setterId preserved. [Re-keyed 2026-06-11: clear moved verbatim (with its anchor comment lines) to StepReview.tsx in the T4.1 split; reviewed by Codex dual-gate.]
